root_text.ud7.9:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā mallesu cārikaṁ caramāno mahatā bhikkhusaṅghena saddhiṁ yena thūṇaṁ nāma mallānaṁ brāhmaṇagāmo tadavasari.
root_text.ud7.9:1.4: “samaṇo khalu, bho, gotamo sakyaputto sakyakulā pabbajito mallesu cārikaṁ caramāno mahatā bhikkhusaṅghena saddhiṁ thūṇaṁ anuppatto”ti.
root_text.ud7.9:1.5: (…) Udapānaṁ tiṇassa ca bhusassa ca yāva mukhato pūresuṁ:
root_text.ud7.9:2.1: Atha kho bhagavā maggā okkamma yena rukkhamūlaṁ tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā paññatte āsane nisīdi.
root_text.ud7.9:2.2: Nisajja kho bhagavā āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ āmantesi:
root_text.ud7.9:2.3: “iṅgha me tvaṁ, ānanda, etamhā udapānā pānīyaṁ āharā”ti.
root_text.ud7.9:3.1: Evaṁ vutte, āyasmā ānando bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.ud7.9:3.2: “idāni so, bhante, udapāno thūṇeyyakehi brāhmaṇagahapatikehi tiṇassa ca bhusassa ca yāva mukhato pūrito:
root_text.ud7.9:4.2: tatiyampi kho bhagavā āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ āmantesi:
root_text.ud7.9:4.3: “iṅgha me tvaṁ, ānanda, etamhā udapānā pānīyaṁ āharā”ti.
root_text.ud7.9:4.4: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti kho āyasmā ānando bhagavato paṭissutvā pattaṁ gahetvā yena so udapāno tenupasaṅkami.
root_text.ud7.9:4.5: Atha kho so udapāno āyasmante ānande upasaṅkamante sabbaṁ taṁ tiṇañca bhusañca mukhato ovamitvā acchassa udakassa anāvilassa vippasannassa yāva mukhato pūrito vissandanto maññe aṭṭhāsi.
root_text.ud7.9:5.2: “acchariyaṁ vata bho, abbhutaṁ vata bho, tathāgatassa mahiddhikatā mahānubhāvatā.
root_text.ud7.9:5.3: Ayañhi so udapāno mayi upasaṅkamante sabbaṁ taṁ tiṇañca bhusañca mukhato ovamitvā acchassa udakassa anāvilassa vippasannassa yāva mukhato pūrito vissandanto maññe ṭhito”ti.
root_text.ud7.9:5.4: Pattena pānīyaṁ ādāya yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.ud7.9:5.5: “acchariyaṁ, bhante, abbhutaṁ, bhante, tathāgatassa mahiddhikatā mahānubhāvatā.
root_text.ud7.9:5.6: Ayañhi so, bhante, udapāno mayi upasaṅkamante sabbaṁ taṁ tiṇañca bhusañca mukhato ovamitvā acchassa udakassa anāvilassa vippasannassa yāva mukhato pūrito vissandanto maññe aṭṭhāsi.
root_text.ud7.9:5.7: Pivatu bhagavā pānīyaṁ, pivatu sugato pānīyan”ti.
root_text.ud7.9:6.1: Atha kho bhagavā etamatthaṁ viditvā tāyaṁ velāyaṁ imaṁ udānaṁ udānesi:

translation_text.ud7.9:1.2: At one time the Buddha was wandering in the land of the Mallas together with a large Saṅgha of mendicants when he arrived at a brahmin town of the Mallas named Thūṇa.
translation_text.ud7.9:1.3: The brahmins and householders of Thūṇa heard:
translation_text.ud7.9:1.4: “It seems the ascetic Gotama—a Sakyan, gone forth from a Sakyan family—while wandering in the land of the Mallas has arrived at Thūṇa, together with a large Saṅgha of mendicants.”
translation_text.ud7.9:1.5: They filled the well with grass and hay right to the top, thinking,
translation_text.ud7.9:1.6: “Don’t let these shavelings, these fake ascetics drink the water.”
translation_text.ud7.9:2.1: And then the Buddha left the road, went to the root of a tree, and sat down on the seat spread out.
translation_text.ud7.9:2.2: When he was seated he said to Venerable Ānanda:
translation_text.ud7.9:2.3: “Go on then, Ānanda, fetch me some water from that well.”
translation_text.ud7.9:3.1: When he said this, Venerable Ānanda said to the Buddha,
translation_text.ud7.9:3.2: “Just now, sir, the brahimns and householders of Thūṇa filled the well with grass and hay right to the top, thinking,
translation_text.ud7.9:3.3: ‘Don’t let these shavelings, these fake ascetics drink the water.’”
translation_text.ud7.9:4.2: and for a third time, the Buddha said to Ānanda:
translation_text.ud7.9:4.3: “Go on then, Ānanda, fetch me some water from that well.”
translation_text.ud7.9:4.4: “Yes, sir,” replied Ānanda. Taking his bowl he went to the well.
translation_text.ud7.9:4.5: As he approached the well, all the grass and hay erupted out of the well-mouth. The water stood transparent, unclouded, and clear right up to the top, seeming to overflow.
translation_text.ud7.9:5.2: “Oh lord, how incredible, how amazing! The Realized One has such psychic power and might!
translation_text.ud7.9:5.3: For when I approached this well, all the grass and hay erupted out of the well-mouth. The water stood transparent, unclouded, and clear right up to the top, seeming to overflow.”
translation_text.ud7.9:5.4: Gathering a bowl of drinking water he went back to the Buddha, and said to him,
translation_text.ud7.9:5.5: “It’s incredible, sir, it’s amazing! The Realized One has such psychic power and might!
translation_text.ud7.9:5.6: For when I approached that well, all the grass and hay erupted out of the well-mouth. The water stood transparent, unclouded, and clear right up to the top, seeming to overflow.
translation_text.ud7.9:5.7: Drink the water, Blessed One! Drink the water, Holy One!”
translation_text.ud7.9:6.1: Then, understanding this matter, on that occasion the Buddha expressed this heartfelt sentiment:
translation_text.ud7.9:7.2: if water is there all the time?
translation_text.ud7.9:7.3: Having cut off craving at the root,
translation_text.ud7.9:7.4: who would go out on a quest?”
